steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The main clause ends at "divergent strategies [blank]." What follows is a "whereas" clause that explains how those strategies split into two regional variants. The blank must connect a complete independent clause to an elaborating explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'emerged and': "and" joins parallel equals, but the "whereas" clause is an elaboration, not a parallel addition. Grammatical mismatch. ✗
  • Choice B — 'emerged' (no punctuation): attaches the elaborating "whereas" clause directly to the main clause with no boundary marker. A separator is required. ✗
  • Choice C — 'emerged,': a comma cannot introduce an elaborating explanation after a complete independent clause — that role belongs to a colon. ✗
  • Choice D — 'emerged:': a colon after an independent clause correctly signals that what follows explains or elaborates — here, showing how the divergent strategies split geographically. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
